---
title: "Advanced Bioinformatics 2026 assessment"
author: "m2503878"
date: "2026-03-18"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

# Task 3.1
```{r}
sum(5:55)
```
# Task 3.2
```{r}
sumfun<-function(n){sum(5:n)}
sumfun(10)
sumfun(20)
sumfun(100)
```
# Task 3.3
```{r}
fibonacci <- function(n) {
  a <- 0
  b <- 1

  cat("Fibonacci Sequence:")
  for (i in 1:n) {
    cat(a, " ")
    next_num <- a + b
    a <- b
    b <- next_num
  }
}

n <- 12
fibonacci(n)
```
# Task 3.4
```{r}
data(mtcars)
library(ggplot2)
mtcars$gear<-as.factor(mtcars$gear)
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = gear, y = mpg, fill = gear)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Miles Per Gallon by Number of Gears",
       x = "Number of Gears",
       y = "Miles Per Gallon (mpg)")


```

#Task 3.5
```{r}
data(cars)
linear_relationship<-lm(cars$dist~cars$speed)
relationshipsummary<-summary(linear_relationship)
relationshipsummary

#intercept is 8.28391 with standard deviation of 0.87438
#slope is 0.16557  with standard deviation of 0.01749

```

#Task 3.6
```{r}
ggplot(cars,aes(x=dist,y=speed))+geom_point()+geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red")+labs(title = "The Breaking distance at increasing speeds", x = "Breaking Distance", y = "Speed")

```

#Task 3.7
```{r}
dist<-cars$dist*0.000189
speed<-cars$speed^2
lm(formula = dist ~ speed)
reactiontime<-2.438e-05^2
reactiontimeseconds<-reactiontime/3600
reactiontime
library(ggplot2)
original<-ggplot(data=cars,aes(speed,dist))+geom_point()
two<-original+geom_smooth(method = "lm",formula = dist ~ speed)
three<-two+ggtitle("linear regression of speed and breaking distance")
three  
```

#Task 3.8
```{r}
counts<-read.csv(file.choose(),row.names=1)
metadata<-read.delim(file.choose())
```

#Task 3.9
```{r}
col_data<-metadata
dim(counts)
dim(col_data)
```

#Task 3.10
```{r}
library(DESeq2)
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts,colData = col_data,design = ~ condition)
```

#Task 3.11
```{r}
rld <- rlog(dds, blind = TRUE)
head(assay(rld))
vsd <- vst(dds, blind = TRUE)
head(assay(vsd))
```

#Task 3.12
```{r}

dds <- estimateSizeFactors(dds)
genes<-order(rowMeans(counts(dds, normalized = TRUE)), decreasing = TRUE)[1:40]
df <- as.data.frame(colData(dds)[, c("condition"), drop = FALSE])
library(pheatmap)
pheatmap(assay(rld)[genes, ], 
         cluster_rows = FALSE, 
         show_rownames = TRUE, 
         cluster_cols = TRUE, 
         annotation_col = df,
         main = "Top 40 Highly Expressed Genes")
```

#Task 3.13
```{r}
sampleDists <- dist(t(assay(vsd)))
sampleDistMatrix <- as.matrix(sampleDists)
sampleDistMatrix
```

#Task 3.14
```{r}
pca_data <- plotPCA(rld, intgroup = "condition", returnData = TRUE)
head(pca_data)
percentVar <- round(100 * attr(pca_data, "percentVar"))
print(paste0("PC1: ", percentVar[1], "% variance"))
print(paste0("PC2: ", percentVar[2], "% variance"))
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(pca_data, aes(PC1, PC2, color = condition)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  xlab(paste0("PC1: ", percentVar[1], "% variance")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC2: ", percentVar[2], "% variance")) +
  coord_fixed() +
  ggtitle("PCA Plot (rlog)")

```

#Task 3.15
```{r}
pca_data_vst <- plotPCA(vsd, intgroup = "condition", returnData = TRUE)
head(pca_data_vst)
percentVar_vst <- round(100 * attr(pca_data_vst, "percentVar"))
print(paste0("PC1: ", percentVar_vst[1], "% variance"))
print(paste0("PC2: ", percentVar_vst[2], "% variance"))
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(pca_data_vst, aes(PC1, PC2, color = condition)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  xlab(paste0("PC1: ", percentVar_vst[1], "% variance")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC2: ", percentVar_vst[2], "% variance")) +
  coord_fixed() +
  ggtitle("PCA Plot (vst)")
```
